command: $before=(Get-FileHash 'C:\mount\Boot\BCD' -Algorithm SHA256).Hash; bcdedit /store C:\mount\Boot\BCD /set {default} testsigning on; bcdedit /store C:\mount\Boot\BCD /set {default} nointegritychecks on; $after=(Get-FileHash 'C:\mount\Boot\BCD' -Algorithm SHA256).Hash; if($before -ne $after){Write-Output 'BCD altered for unsigned code'}; certutil -hashfile C:\mount\Boot\BCD SHA256
explanation: Modifies the offline BCD store in the system image to permit unsigned code and verifies the alteration via hash comparison, an example of system image patching consistent with T1601.
rationale_steps: Hash the offline BCD store as a baseline.
Set testsigning and disable integrity checks in the BCD of the image.
Log the BCD hash with certutil for records.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell line is well-formed with balanced delimiters and valid flags, but {default} is unquoted and will be parsed as a scriptblock in PowerShell, requiring quoting/escaping to be reliably passed to bcdedit.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The approach appropriately targets the offline BCD store to weaken boot integrity (consistent with T1601) and verifies changes via hashes, but it will likely fail due to unquoted {default} in PowerShell and generally requires elevated privileges despite the 'Privilege: None' claim.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed PowerShell with balanced parentheses, proper semicolons as separators, valid bcdedit flags, correct Get-FileHash and certutil syntax, and properly quoted paths throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements offline BCD store modification to disable integrity checks and enable test signing (weakening boot security), uses appropriate tools (bcdedit, Get-FileHash, certutil) for the task, performs before/after hash comparison to verify tampering, and is fully consistent with T1601 system image patching to weaken defenses.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses proper PowerShell syntax, including correct variable assignments, command separators, and balanced braces/parentheses.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately uses bcdedit to modify an offline BCD store to disable integrity checks, a textbook example of patching a system image for persistence or defense evasion.
